title: 2026 U.S. House race for Michigan's 7th Congressional District
description: Michigan’s 7th Congressional District race features Democratic candidates Elyon Badger, Bridget Brink, and Matt Maasdam. The seat is currently held by Republican Tom Barrett.
polling_note: Polling data is incomplete and not published in this profile.

candidates.name: Bridget Brink
candidates.summary: Bridget Brink is a Michigan native, sixth-generation Michigander, and former U.S. ambassador to Ukraine who is running as a Democrat in Michigan’s 7th Congressional District. Her campaign emphasizes her foreign-service background, her resignation from the ambassador post over U.S. policy toward Ukraine, and her focus on lowering costs, health care, and defending democratic institutions.

summary_sources.title: Meet Bridget - Bridget Brink for Congress
Former U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine announces run for Michigan's 7th Congressional District

candidates.donor_summary: Bridget Brink’s 2026 House campaign reported $1,643,666.77 in total receipts through 12/31/2025, with $1,603,216.65 from individual contributions and $20,450 from other committees; the campaign also reported a $20,000 candidate loan. Public FEC summary data does not identify top industries or named major donors on the candidate overview page, but the campaign’s own site says it is powered by grassroots support and reported over $2.2M raised by early April 2026.
candidates.voting_summary: Bridget Brink has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. She previously served as U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine (2022–2025), and her campaign frames her governing approach around affordability, health care, Social Security and Medicare, democracy, the environment, schools, and rights.

links.title: Candidate Bio - Bridget Brink — Leadership Now Project
Ambassador to Ukraine who resigned under Trump administration could run for Congress - CBS News
Former US ambassador to Ukraine who resigned in protest ... - AP News
Meet Bridget - Bridget Brink for Congress

Healthcare.stance: Brink supports extending ACA tax credits, creating a public health insurance option, repealing Medicaid cuts, lowering prescription drug costs, and capping insulin at $35. She also backs stronger consumer protections and more transparency from insurers and drug companies.

sources.title: Making Health Care More Affordable and Accessible
Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

Economy.stance: Brink’s economic plan focuses on lowering costs for families, supporting small businesses, investing in infrastructure and broadband, expanding job training and free community college, and strengthening worker rights. She also says she would roll back tariffs, reform zoning and permitting to expand housing, and crack down on corporate landlords and utility monopolies.

sources.title: Lowering Costs and Investing in mid-Michigan

Climate/Energy.stance: Brink says she supports protecting the environment and growing the green economy. Her campaign also says it would increase regulation of public utility monopolies and support clean, future-oriented economic development.

sources.title: Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress
Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

Reproductive Rights.stance: Brink says she supports protecting fundamental rights and freedoms, and her campaign materials place reproductive freedom within that broader rights framework. Public campaign materials reviewed do not provide a more detailed abortion policy statement on the main issues pages beyond that rights-based framing.

sources.title: Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress
Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

Guns & Safety.stance: No public position found on guns or gun safety in the campaign materials reviewed.

sources.title: Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

Foreign Policy.stance: Brink’s foreign-policy message centers on supporting Ukraine, defending democracy, and opposing Russian aggression. She says her diplomatic experience shaped her view that the U.S. should remain engaged with allies and stand against corruption and authoritarianism.

sources.title: Meet Bridget - Bridget Brink for Congress
Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

Social Justice.stance: Brink frames her campaign around protecting fundamental rights and freedoms, defending democracy, and making health care and other essentials more affordable. Her materials also emphasize her union background and support for working families.

sources.title: Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress
Meet Bridget - Bridget Brink for Congress

Education.stance: Brink supports strengthening public schools and expanding job training, career and technical education, and free community college. Her campaign says education and workforce development are part of preparing workers for the future economy.

sources.title: Lowering Costs and Investing in mid-Michigan
Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

Tech & AI.stance: Brink says she supports clear rules for emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, to encourage innovation while protecting consumers from risk and bad actors. Her campaign specifically says it would crack down on corporate landlords who use A.I. to artificially raise rents and hide fees.

sources.title: Lowering Costs and Investing in mid-Michigan

Election Reform.stance: Brink says she supports fighting corruption and defending democracy, including stronger ethics rules and accountability for public officials. Her campaign also emphasizes protecting the integrity of elections as part of that broader democracy message.

sources.title: Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress
Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

Local Issues.stance: Brink’s local priorities for mid-Michigan focus on lowering costs, making health care more affordable, protecting Social Security and Medicare, strengthening public schools, defending democracy, and growing the green economy. Her campaign also highlights infrastructure, broadband, housing, and small-business support.

sources.title: Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress
Lowering Costs and Investing in mid-Michigan

Immigration.stance: No public position found on immigration in the campaign materials reviewed.

sources.title: Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress
Priorities - Bridget Brink for Congress

candidates.name: Matt Maasdam
candidates.summary: Matt Maasdam is a former Navy SEAL, White House military aide, and entrepreneur running as a Democrat in Michigan’s 7th Congressional District. His campaign emphasizes military service, business experience, and practical problem-solving focused on affordability.

career_history.title: Entrepreneur
career_history.description: Campaign materials describe him as a business owner and entrepreneur.

career_history.title: Military Aide to the President
career_history.description: Served as President Barack Obama’s military aide, coordinating emergency response planning and carrying the nuclear football.

career_history.title: Executive
career_history.description: Worked in Under Armour's e-commerce operations after leaving active duty; campaign and reporting describe him as a former Under Armour executive.

career_history.title: Founder
career_history.description: Founded two e-commerce businesses after leaving the military and corporate sector.

career_history.title: Navy SEAL
career_history.description: Served as a Navy SEAL from 2000 to 2013, including a White House military aide assignment during that period.

candidates.donor_summary: Matt Maasdam’s campaign reported more than $1 million raised in 2025, including $400,000 in Q4, according to the campaign website. The public FEC summary page shows receipts and transfers but does not list major named donors or top industries on the candidate overview page.
candidates.voting_summary: Matt Maasdam has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. His public record is instead as a Navy SEAL, White House military aide, and business executive.

links.title: Matt Maasdam runs as Democrat for U.S. House against Tom Barrett - Detroit Free Press
Former Democratic Michigan congressman backs Maasdam's 7th district bid - Michigan Advance

Healthcare.stance: Matt Maasdam supports protecting Medicaid and the Affordable Care Act, including extending ACA tax credits, lowering prescription drug costs, capping out-of-pocket costs, and allowing people to buy into Medicare. His campaign has also criticized efforts that would make healthcare coverage more expensive.

sources.title: Matt Maasdam for Congress
Maasdam Slams Barrett for Letting Critical ACA Tax Credits Expire

Economy.stance: Matt Maasdam says his economic focus is lowering everyday costs for working families. His campaign highlights support for reducing healthcare costs, cutting red tape to build more housing, rolling back tariffs that raise prices, and expanding childcare.

sources.title: Matt Maasdam for Congress
Priorities - Matt Maasdam for Congress

Climate/Energy.stance: No public position found in the campaign materials reviewed.

Guns & Safety.stance: No public position found in the campaign materials reviewed.

sources.title: Matt Maasdam for Congress

Foreign Policy.stance: Matt Maasdam’s background is in military service and national security, including service as a Navy SEAL and White House military aide. However, his campaign materials do not publish a detailed foreign policy platform.

sources.title: Matt Maasdam for Congress

Social Justice.stance: Matt Maasdam says he will fight for civil rights and LGBTQ rights as part of defending democracy and freedom.

sources.title: Priorities - Matt Maasdam for Congress

Education.stance: Matt Maasdam says he supports fully funding public schools so every child, regardless of ZIP code, has a chance to succeed.

sources.title: Priorities - Matt Maasdam for Congress

Tech & AI.stance: Matt Maasdam says Michigan should lead in emerging industries such as AI and digital assets. His campaign supports clear, commonsense rules for digital assets that protect consumers while encouraging responsible innovation, and it says families should be protected from harmful content and privacy risks tied to AI.

sources.title: Priorities - Matt Maasdam for Congress
Matt Maasdam for Congress

Election Reform.stance: Matt Maasdam supports anti-corruption and election-reform measures, including banning stock trading by members of Congress and senior government officials, rejecting corporate PAC money, and reducing the influence of dark money in elections.

sources.title: Matt Maasdam for Congress
End Citizens United backs Maasdam in race for Michigan's 7th Congressional District

Reproductive Rights.stance: Matt Maasdam says he will fight for women’s rights, including the right to choose.

sources.title: Priorities - Matt Maasdam for Congress

Local Issues.stance: Matt Maasdam’s campaign emphasizes lowering everyday costs in Michigan, including health care, housing, groceries, and childcare, along with protecting manufacturing and public schools.

sources.title: Priorities - Matt Maasdam for Congress

candidates.name: Elyon Badger
candidates.summary: Elyon Badger, who also uses the name Samuel Smeltzer, is a Democratic candidate in Michigan’s 7th Congressional District. His campaign describes him as a Michigan Army National Guard veteran, LGBTQIA+ activist, and small business owner, and his platform includes universal healthcare, ending the drug war, and a Technology Bill of Rights.

summary_sources.title: Elyon Badger for Congress – MI-07 – Issues

career_history.title: Michigan Army National Guard veteran
career_history.description: Campaign materials identify him as a veteran of the Michigan Army National Guard.

career_history.title: Small business owner
career_history.description: Campaign materials identify him as a small business owner.

career_history.title: LGBTQIA+ activist
career_history.description: Campaign materials identify him as an LGBTQIA+ activist.

candidates.donor_summary: Elyon Badger’s FEC candidate page currently shows no public 2025–2026 financial activity for the committee ELYON BADGER FOR CONGRESS (C00909002), so there are no reported receipts or named major donors to summarize from the public disclosure page. This means the available finance record is presently sparse rather than absent from the race entirely.
candidates.voting_summary: Elyon Badger has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. His public record is as a Michigan Army National Guard veteran, activist, and small business owner, and his campaign site lays out policy positions on universal health care, drug legalization, rail/highway infrastructure, anti-corruption, technology rights, student debt cancellation, and immigration enforcement. Confidence: high.

flags.concern: Polling matchups contain null percentages and lack substantive data. The source URL (Public Policy Polling article from November 2025) is cited but the actual polling numbers are not populated in the profile. The polling_note correctly flags this as 'incomplete,' but the presence of empty matchup objects may confuse users.
flags.suggestion: Either remove empty matchup objects and consolidate into a single note that no usable polling data was recovered, or populate the percentages from the source. If the source URL does not contain the numbers, remove the polling section entirely and replace with a single narrative note.

flags.concern: The claim that Bridget Brink 'resigned from the ambassador post over U.S. policy toward Ukraine' is sourced to her campaign website, but this phrasing may conflate two separate facts: her resignation under Trump and her disagreement with Trump policy. The cited news sources (CBS, AP) confirm she resigned in protest, so the claim is accurate as sourced, but human review of those articles would confirm the exact framing of her reasoning.
flags.suggestion: The sourcing is adequate; this is an 'info' flag only. No change necessary unless human review of AP/CBS articles suggests the phrasing should be more precise.

flags.concern: The donor_summary states 'more than $1 million raised in 2025' and '$400,000 in Q4, according to the campaign website,' but cites the FEC page as source_url. FEC is the authoritative source for campaign finance; if the campaign website claims differ from FEC, that discrepancy should be noted. The summary appears to prioritize campaign-claimed figures over the official FEC record.
flags.suggestion: Clarify in the donor_summary which figures come from the campaign website vs. FEC official records. Cross-check FEC page H6MI07272 to confirm the figures align with official disclosures.

flags.concern: The profile notes that Elyon Badger 'also uses the name Samuel Smeltzer' and the FEC page is titled 'SMELTZER BADGER, SAMUEL ELYON.' This is accurate per the FEC record, but the profile does not clarify whether this is a legal name change, a stage name, or a chosen professional name. This is important context for readers.
flags.suggestion: Add a brief clarification in the summary (e.g., 'Badger's FEC registration lists his name as Samuel Elyon Smeltzer Badger') to explain the name relationship without speculation about its nature.

flags.concern: The donor_summary correctly reports that 'Elyon Badger's FEC candidate page currently shows no public 2025–2026 financial activity,' which is appropriate transparency. However, the parenthetical note 'so there are no reported receipts or named major donors to summarize from the public disclosure page' could be misread as implying no campaign finance activity occurred—when in fact it simply means no disclosures were filed or found. This is a minor clarity issue, not a factual error.
flags.suggestion: Rephrase to: 'Elyon Badger's FEC candidate page shows no filed or disclosed 2025–2026 financial activity for committee C00909002. Without filings, no receipt or donor data are available to summarize.' This makes clear that absence of data reflects absence of filings, not absence of funding.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates strong sourcing and factual accuracy across all three Democratic candidates in Michigan's 7th Congressional District. Each candidate's background, career history, and policy positions are supported by credible sources: official campaign websites, FEC filings, news reports from reputable outlets (MLive, Detroit Free Press, AP News, CBS News), and third-party trackers (Ballotpedia, VoteVets). The profile appropriately handles the absence of legislative voting records for all three non-incumbent candidates, instead documenting their stated policy positions with high-confidence citations. Language is neutral and nonpartisan throughout. Minor gaps exist in polling data completeness and some candidates' positions on certain issues (climate, immigration, guns), but these are correctly marked as "low confidence" after good-faith search, which is appropriate and not a quality failure. The profile accurately reflects Bridget Brink's ambassador background, Matt Maasdam's Navy SEAL and White House service, and Elyon Badger's activist and military background as documented in retrieved sources.

flags.concern: The career history and education arrays for Bridget Brink are empty, although her professional background as an ambassador is well-detailed in the summary and voting_summary fields.
flags.suggestion: Populate the structured career_history and education arrays using the information already present in the summary and sources (e.g., U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine).

flags.concern: The education array for Elyon Badger is empty.
flags.suggestion: If education data was not found during the search, this is acceptable, but worth a final check of the official campaign 'About' page to ensure no degrees were missed.

reviews.summary: This is an exceptionally thorough and well-documented profile for the 2026 Michigan 7th District race. The research successfully captures a diverse field of candidates—ranging from a high-level diplomat to a grassroots activist—while maintaining a neutral, matter-of-fact tone throughout. Sourcing is top-tier, utilizing live FEC filings, specific campaign policy sub-pages, and reputable news outlets like AP and the Detroit Free Press to provide granular detail on issues like the 'Technology Bill of Rights' and specific healthcare caps.

This profile is a well-constructed and reliable overview of the 2026 Michigan 7th Congressional District race, with strong sourcing from official campaign websites, FEC records, and credible news outlets like MLive, CBS, and AP News, ensuring high-confidence stances on key issues such as healthcare and foreign policy. Candidate backgrounds are consistently documented with direct citations, and the profile neutrally handles absences of public positions by noting them explicitly, demonstrating thorough research effort without introducing bias.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 3/3 reviewers with an average score of 91/100.
